intended_use: AI-Rad Companion (Musculoskeletal) is an image processing software that provides quantitative and qualitative analysis from previously acquired Computed Tomography DICOM images to support radiologists
intended_use_full: AI-Rad Companion (Musculoskeletal) is an image processing software that provides quantitative and qualitative analysis from previously acquired Computed Tomography DICOM images to support radiologists and physicians from emergency medicine, specialty care, urgent care, and general practice in the evaluation and assessment of musculoskeletal disease. It provides the following functionality: Segmentation of vertebras, Labelling of vertebras, Measurements of heights in each vertebra and indication if they are critically different, Measurement of mean Hounsfield value in volume of interest within vertebra.
ground_truth: Manual measurements of vertebral heights and density (HU values) by four board-certified radiologists with consensus methodology (average of two most concordant measurements or mean of two independent annotations)
other_metrics: Mislabeling/absence of height measurement failure rate: 8.6%; Inter-reader variability for cases with slice thickness ≤1.0 mm: 95.5% within limits of agreement; Inter-reader variability for cases with slice thickness >1.0 mm: 92.6% within limits of agreement
